pages.title: Welcome
pages.text:
This is the guide to Ala Industrial — EU energy, generators, machines and cables.

Pick a section on the left, turn pages with the arrows. The "Open Wiki" button up top opens the full guide site.

pages.title: First steps
pages.text:
1. Mine the mod's ore — start with tin.
2. Place a Solar Panel — the simplest generator.
3. Run a copper cable to it.
4. Hook up your first machine — the Macerator.

Details are in the Energy and Processing sections.

pages.title: EU energy
pages.text: EU (Energy Unit) is the mod's unit of energy. Generators produce EU, cables transmit it, storage holds it, machines spend it. It is a separate system from vanilla redstone.

pages.title: Voltage tiers
pages.text:
Energy flows in packets capped by a per-tick voltage:
• LV — up to 32 EU/tick (basic blocks)
• MV — up to 128 EU/tick
• HV — up to 512 EU/tick

A tier's limit is throughput per tick, not a break threshold: blocks are not destroyed by 'overvoltage'.

pages.title: Cables
pages.text: Cables join blocks into a power grid and carry EU. The copper cable loses energy over distance — the longer the run, the more is lost. Keep runs short, or place storage closer to the consumer.

pages.title: Wind Turbine
pages.text: A basic LV wind turbine — generates EU from wind with no fuel; build it higher and in foul weather for more power.

pages.title: Water Mill
pages.text: A passive LV generator — produces EU from water around the block, with no fuel or tanks.

pages.title: Generator
pages.text: A simple coal-and-solid-fuel generator — the starter LV EU source for your first machines.

pages.title: Geothermal Generator
pages.text: Generates EU from lava — stronger than the coal generator and needs refilling far less often.

pages.title: Daylight Solar Panel
pages.text: The T2 day panel — four times stronger than the base, runs by day under open sky; obtained only by evolution.

pages.title: Moonlit Solar Panel
pages.text: The T2 night panel — generates EU at night under open sky and even trickles in bad weather; obtained only by evolution.

pages.title: Solar Panel
pages.text: The mod's first power source — generates EU by day under open sky; where the whole energy chain begins.

pages.title: Battery Box
pages.text: The starter LV storage block — holds 20 000 EU, banks surplus from a generator and feeds it to machines at 32 EU/tick.

pages.title: Insulated Copper Cable
pages.text: A copper LV cable wrapped in wool — for safe adjacency and tight wiring next to machines.

pages.title: Insulated Tin Cable
pages.text: A tin LV cable wrapped in wool — the cheapest insulated wire for safe adjacency.

pages.title: Copper Cable
pages.text: A cheap, workhorse LV cable for early energy networks — links solar panels, batteries and machines into a single line.

pages.title: Tin Cable
pages.text: The cheapest LV cable — for thin, low-power lines to single machines and weak generators.

pages.title: Macerator
pages.text: Crushes ore (both blocks and raw ore) into two dusts, spending EU at the LV tier.

pages.title: Iron Furnace
pages.text: An upgraded fuel furnace between vanilla and the Electric Furnace — faster than vanilla, still fuel-based, no EU.

pages.title: Compressor
pages.text: Squeeze raw materials into denser forms — metal dust into ingots, coal/gem dust back into items, ice into packed/blue ice, sugar cane into paper. Closes the doubling loop for non-metals.

pages.title: Extractor
pages.text: Pulls useful material out of raw inputs — ×2 dye from flowers, more seeds from pumpkin/melon, blaze powder from rods, flint from gravel. Handy for hopper-fed auto-farms.

pages.title: Electric Furnace
pages.text: Smelts everything the vanilla furnace does, plus the mod's dusts into ingots. Runs on EU instead of fuel.

pages.title: Pump
pages.text: An LV pump that drains fluid from the world or adjacent tanks into its 10-bucket internal tank and pushes it on to receivers; exchanges fluid with capsules and other mods' containers.

pages.title: Item Pipe
pages.text: Passive multipart pipe that transfers items between inventories.

pages.title: Portable Fluid Tank
pages.text: A passive 8-bucket tank with a visible fluid level, six-sided fluid access, and contents retained when moved.

pages.title: Block of Tempered Iron
pages.text: A compact block of 9 tempered-iron ingots — stores the metal in one slot and is crafted or uncrafted at a workbench with no loss.

pages.title: Nickel Ore
pages.text: The rarest of the mod's metals — ore in the world and the raw → dust → ingot chain; the material for MV alloys and machine casings.

pages.title: Tin Ore
pages.text: An early mod metal — ore in the world and the raw → dust → ingot chain; the base material for LV wires and circuits.

pages.title: Silver Ore
pages.text: A rare mod ore metal — ore block and the raw → dust → ingot chain; the material for mid-tier circuits and components.

pages.title: Uranium Ore
pages.text: The mod's rarest ore — mined with an iron pickaxe, processed into dust and ingot; reserved for future nuclear machines.

pages.title: Teleporter
pages.text: An HV anchor station and a handheld remote — bound via shift-right-click, teleporting the player to a station for a large EU cost with a warmup, safe landing, and privacy protection.

pages.title: Iron Chest
pages.text: A 36-slot chest — one row more than vanilla, with a 3D model, animated lid and its own sound. Needs no energy.

pages.title: Gold Chest
pages.text: A 54-slot chest — the tier above silver (one more row), with a 3D model and animated lid. Needs no energy.

pages.title: Guide Book
pages.text: An in-game tutorial book covering every mechanic of the mod — auto-given on a player's first join, opened with a right-click; tabs, pages, links. No energy.

pages.title: Player Profile
pages.text: A profile screen with career mod statistics and a mastery progression (40 levels, 8 ranks) that levels up from machine work and, far more slowly, from generator output.

pages.title: Stats
lines: Energy: 1 000 EU of machine work = 1 mastery point

pages.title: Enriched Uranium Torch
pages.text: A decorative torch with a green flame and light level 14 — it even burns underwater. Needs no energy.

pages.title: Stock Display Frame
pages.text: A frame you hang on a chest — it shows the item count inside, right on the frame. No energy needed; the number is only visible with a filter item placed in the frame.

pages.title: Silver Chest
pages.text: A 45-slot chest — the tier above iron (one more row), with a 3D model and animated lid. Needs no energy.

pages.title: Mute Chip
pages.text: An upgrade that fully silences a machine — goes into the upgrade slot.

pages.title: Battery
pages.text: An inert energy cell — the blank from which the Energy Pack is crafted; holds no EU on its own.

pages.title: Water Wheel
pages.text: Water mill wheel — placed in the wheel slot of a water mill; without it, no generation. Not consumed while in slot.

pages.title: Tempered Iron
pages.text: An ingot smelted from a vanilla iron ingot — the base material for the mod's tools, armor and block.

pages.title: Copper Coil
pages.text: A crafting component — copper cable wound on a tin core; gates the Electric Drill.

pages.title: Iron Gear
pages.text: The gear family — stone, iron, gold and silver; crafting components for machinery still to come.

pages.title: Empty Chip
pages.text: The blank base for machine upgrade chips; has no effect on its own.

pages.title: Wooden Rotor
pages.text: Wind turbine blades — placed in the rotor slot of any T1/T2 wind mill; without it, no generation. Not consumed while in slot.

pages.title: Network Analyzer
pages.text: A hand-held diagnostic tool — right-click a cable to view that power network's stats and highlight it in the world.

pages.title: Iron Dust
pages.text: Dust and related intermediates (plate, billet) — what the Macerator and other machines turn ores into.

pages.title: Solar Evolution Chip
pages.text: A pair of evolution chips — solar (day) and lunar (night) — that evolve basic T1 solar panels and wind mills into T2 via evolution, carrying accumulated NBT energy over.

pages.title: Electronic Circuit
pages.text: The base crafting component — the 'brain' of nearly every LV machine and storage block.

pages.title: Battery Pouch
pages.text: A chargeable item pouch — holds 128 weight, runs on a built-in 2000 EU battery and locks when it runs out of charge.

pages.title: Tempered Iron Chestplate
pages.text: A full tempered-iron armor set — helmet, chestplate, leggings and boots; tougher than iron, repaired with tempered iron.

pages.title: Vacuum Capsule
pages.text: A stackable one-bucket fluid container — up to 16 capsules of one fluid per stack, filled from the world or the mod's tanks.

pages.title: Wrench
pages.text: Configures Item Pipe faces and dismantles the mod's blocks.

pages.title: Jetpack
pages.text: A chest-slot EU flight device — thrust while jump is held, a soft powerless glide when drained.

pages.title: Tempered Iron Pickaxe
pages.text: A hand-held tempered-iron pickaxe — slightly better than vanilla iron in every stat, but it cannot mine diamond-level blocks.

pages.title: Tempered Iron Tools
pages.text: A set of five hand tools made of tempered iron — pickaxe, axe, hoe, shovel and sword; slightly better than vanilla iron, but they cannot mine diamond-level blocks.

pages.title: Scythe
pages.text: An AOE tool that clears leaves, grass, flowers and other decorative foliage in a single swing, and with Shift held — an AOE sickle that harvests ripe crops. Eight material tiers from wood to netherite.

pages.title: Teleporter Remote
pages.text: A handheld beacon-remote — binds to a teleporter station via shift-right-click and triggers a jump to it for EU paid by the station.

pages.title: Electric Drill
pages.text: A rechargeable diamond-tier pickaxe that runs on EU — mines like diamond while charged, spends EU per block, never breaks.

pages.title: Electromagnet
pages.text: An EU-rechargeable item in any inventory slot that draws nearby loose drops toward the player, spending EU per item.

pages.title: Energy Pack
pages.text: A wearable EU buffer in the chest slot — 20 000 EU that keeps the powered items in your inventory topped up.
